themes.headline: QAAUM ₹9.3 trillion, up 20% YoY; equity-oriented AUM at ₹6 trillion
themes.detail: Industry as a whole saw ₹7.4 trillion of net inflows in FY26, the 14th consecutive year of positive net inflows.

themes.headline: Unique investor share moved from 17.6% to 27% over three years
themes.detail: Added ~10 million unique investors over three years; total accounts now exceed 30 million; HDFC alone added 3.5 of the industry's 7.2 million net-new investors in FY26.

themes.headline: Digital transactions hit 97% — up from 81% three years back
themes.detail: Management framed the firm as a 'digital AI value creator', not a traditional asset manager.

themes.headline: TER → BER framework live April 1 2026; gross 3-4 bps P&L hit
themes.detail: 5 bps incentive in lieu of exit loads removed. Plan is to offset through commission optimisation and tighter direct/indirect cost control.

themes.headline: Alternatives: first close of private credit fund with IFC anchor
themes.detail: GiftCity has 5 live funds. PMS won EPFO + Siemens PF mandates this year. SIF approach explicitly framed as 'deliberate, not a race'.

themes.headline: Employee costs +12.5% YoY ex-ESOPs; 5Y CAGR at 13%
themes.detail: Headcount grew from 1,250 to 1,700 over 5 years. Cost / AAUM has trended down despite continued tech, talent and new-vertical investment.

themes.headline: 98% of India zip-codes contribute inflows; P30 towns are the growth engine
themes.detail: Open architecture across HDFC Bank, MFDs, national distributors, and fintech. Fintech flow share now exceeds its book share.

channelMix.note: Direct plans ~31% of equity AUM; open architecture; fintech flow share exceeds book share

events.label: Private credit AIF — first close with IFC as anchor investor
Two new inbound funds in GiftCity; total now at 5
Seven new mutual fund schemes launched during the year
Rajan Anandan (Peak XV) joins technology committee as invitee

quotes.text: We are transitioning from a traditional asset manager to a digital AI value creator.
quotes.speaker: Navneet Munot, MD & CEO
